name: Flipkart Product Catalog — GraphQL Schema Design
scenario: Flipkart's frontend team is tired of making 6 API calls per product page. Design a GraphQL schema that lets the web app, iOS app, and Android app each request exactly the fields they need in a single query.

core.description: Product type: id (ID!), name (String!), basePrice (Float!), discountedPrice (Float, nullable), category (Category!), seller (Seller!), images ([String!]!), rating (Float, nullable), inStock (Boolean!), reviews ([Review!]!).
Category type: id, name, parent (Category, nullable — top-level has none), children ([Category!]!). Category forms a self-referential tree.
Seller type: id, name, rating, deliveryDays, verified. Review type: id, rating (Int!, 1-5), comment (String, nullable), authorName.

Input types: CreateProductInput (name!, basePrice!, categoryId!, sellerId!), ProductFilter (all nullable fields: minPrice, maxPrice, minRating, inStock, categoryId). ProductSort: field (enum) + direction (ASC/DESC enum).
Pagination type: ProductPage { items: [Product!]!, total: Int!, page: Int!, hasNext: Boolean! }. Query: products(filter, sort, page=0, size=20) returns ProductPage.
Query type: product(id), products(filter, sort, page, size), categories(parentId), searchProducts(keyword, filter, page, size), featuredProducts.
Mutation type: createProduct, updateProductPrice(productId, newPrice). Use input type for createProduct.

4 client queries (in SDL comment or separate file): home page (featured + categories), category listing (products with pagination), product detail (all fields + variants), search query. @SpringBootTest (3 tests): schema contains expected types, queries use fragments, evolution documented. All pass.

bonus.description: ProductVariant type: id, color (nullable), size (nullable), price, stock. Add variants: [ProductVariant!]! to Product. Write a query fetching variants.
SearchResult union: Product | Category | Seller. Query: search(keyword) returns [SearchResult!]!. Client query with __typename and inline fragments.
Custom scalar: Rupee (Long paise). Document serialization: ₹5000 = 500000 paise. Add to schema with scalar Rupee and update price fields to use it.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. Non-null design: use ID! for all entity IDs, String! for required names, Float! for prices (always present). Use nullable (no !) for: optional fields (description, discount), single-entity queries (product(id): Product, not Product!). Input types vs object types: mutations take input types (input keyword), return object types (type keyword) — you cannot use Product as a mutation argument. Pagination pattern: ProductPage { items, total, page, hasNext } — more scalable than returning [Product!]! directly (allows adding totalPages, cursor later). Self-referential type: Category.parent: Category (nullable — top-level categories have no parent). Safe schema changes: add nullable fields freely; never remove fields without @deprecated first.
